user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

queries:
Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

enrichment_log: Section 65 Indian Contract: FOUND in RAG

placeholder_dates.AGREEMENT_DATE: Date of the agreement/contract between parties
placeholder_dates.ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE: Date when the plaintiff made the payment/advance
placeholder_dates.PERFORMANCE_DATE: Date by which the defendant was obligated to perform
placeholder_dates.DEFAULT_DATE: Date when the defendant failed to repay/perform
placeholder_dates.NOTICE_DATE: Date when legal notice was sent
placeholder_dates.NOTICE_REPLY_DATE: Date when reply to legal notice was received
placeholder_dates.CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE: Date when cause of action first arose

sections.body:
SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

sections.heading: BETWEEN
sections.instruction: Draft the parties section listing plaintiff and defendant(s) with full particulars: name, age, occupation, and address. Use sui juris averment for competency. If any detail is missing, use the appropriate {{PLACEHOLDER}}.

sections.heading: JURISDICTION
sections.instruction: Draft the jurisdiction section establishing territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction. For territorial jurisdiction, state SPECIFIC facts: where the Defendant resides or carries on business, where payment was made, where the agreement was executed, or where the cause of action arose — with concrete place references. For pecuniary jurisdiction, state the claim amount and that it falls within this court's monetary limits.

sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
sections.instruction: Draft the facts section as numbered paragraphs in chronological narrative form. Each paragraph must tell a story — not state conclusions. Cover: (1) How the parties came to know each other and the nature of their relationship, (2) What was agreed — the specific terms of the transaction/agreement, (3) What obligations each party undertook and the timeline for performance, (4) How payment was made — specific amount, mode, date, (5) What exactly constituted the defendant's failure — describe the specific default with detail, (6) What happened after the default — communications, demands, legal notice and response. Reference documents as 'Annexure [LABEL]'. Do NOT state legal conclusions in this section — keep it purely factual.

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
sections.instruction: Draft the legal basis section in paragraph form. For each statutory provision: (1) State what the provision says — the legal rule it creates, (2) Explain what factual conditions trigger its application, (3) Show how the facts of this case satisfy those conditions. If applicable, include an alternative plea (e.g., Section 73 ICA as alternative to Section 65 ICA) using 'In the alternative and without prejudice to the above...' framing. Include defensive pleading: pre-emptively neutralize likely defense arguments (e.g., no part performance was rendered, no forfeiture clause exists, no adjustment made, no counter-claim exists). Cite ONLY provisions from the verified list.

must_include.description: At least one statutory section cited
Legal consequence explained (not just section named)

sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
sections.instruction: Draft the cause of action section in PARAGRAPH prose (not numbered sub-headings). Must cover: (1) ORIGIN — when and how the right to sue first arose, anchored to the default/failure date, (2) FURTHER ACCRUAL — when the cause of action further arose (e.g., failure to comply with legal notice), (3) CONTINUITY — that the cause of action is continuing because the defendant continues to wrongfully retain the money. The cause must be anchored to the failure/default date, NOT the notice date. Write in flowing paragraph form — avoid mechanical sub-headings like '(a) underlying obligation'.

sections.heading: LIMITATION
sections.instruction: Draft the limitation section stating: (1) the applicable Article of the Limitation Act, 1963 Schedule (from enrichment or use placeholder), (2) the limitation period, (3) the starting date of limitation (must be anchored to cause_of_action_date/default_date, NOT notice_date), (4) that the suit is within time. If limitation article is not available in enrichment, use {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} placeholder.

sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
sections.instruction: Draft the valuation and court fee section stating: (1) suit valuation equals the claim amount, (2) court fee calculated per applicable state court fees act. Use court_fee context from web search if available, otherwise use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder.

sections.heading: INTEREST
sections.instruction: Draft the interest section with a 3-part structure: (1) Pre-suit interest — from default date to filing date with rate and justification, (2) Pendente lite interest — during pendency of suit under Section 34 CPC with rate, (3) Future interest — post-decree until realization with rate. Justify the interest rate: state why the claimed rate is appropriate (commercial transaction, wrongful retention, prevailing bank rate, contractual understanding). Cite Section 34 of CPC for pendente lite interest.

sections.heading: PRAYER
sections.instruction: Draft the prayer section with at least 6 specific sub-prayers as lettered items (a, b, c, ...). Each prayer must state exact amounts or rate placeholders — never vague. Must include: (a) decree for principal amount with Rs. figure, (b) pre-suit interest with rate and date range, (c) pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC with rate, (d) future interest post-decree with rate until realization, (e) costs of the suit including pre-litigation costs for sending legal notice, (f) any other relief the court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances.

sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
sections.instruction: Generate a numbered list of documents the plaintiff relies upon, mapping to the annexure scheme. For each document: Serial No., Description, Annexure Label. Only list documents mentioned in evidence. If evidence is empty, list standard expected documents with {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders.

sections.heading: VERIFICATION
sections.body:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

filled_sections.text:
SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

filled_sections.heading: BETWEEN
filled_sections.text: Between Plaintiff and Defendant, the Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, is a sui juris person competent to sue and be sued. The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, is a sui juris person competent to be sued.

filled_sections.heading: JURISDICTION
filled_sections.text:
The territorial jurisdiction of this Court is founded on direct, concrete connections between the parties and the events giving rise to the dispute. The Defendant resides and carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which lies within the territorial jurisdiction of this Court. The advance amount was transferred by the Plaintiff from its account at {{BANK_NAME}} {{BANK_BRANCH}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, within the local limits of this Court's jurisdiction. The agreement was executed at {{AGREEMENT_PLACE}} on {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, and the contract is performed within the same territorial footprint. The cause of action arose at {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_PLACE}} on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} when the Defendant allegedly defaulted on repayment and performance. Place: {{PLACE}}.

Pecuniary jurisdiction is invoked for recovery of the money claim. The Plaintiff claims Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}, which falls within this Court's monetary limits.

filled_sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
filled_sections.text:
Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, and Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, first became acquainted in the course of their regular commercial dealings, and subsequently entered into a written contract dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} (Annexure A) under which the Plaintiff agreed to furnish funds to the Defendant for a stated business purpose and the Defendant agreed to accept the funds and perform the obligations set out in Annexure A. The relationship between the parties thus was that of a financier and borrower arising from a formalized financing arrangement documented in Annexure A.

The terms of the agreement, as reflected in Annexure A, contemplated that the Plaintiff would advance funds to the Defendant for the specified purpose and that the Defendant would repay the principal amount on the predetermined due date and adhere to the other conditions described in Annexure A, including any remedies available on breach. The contract also contained the condition that all terms would be binding and enforceable against the parties in the event of non-performance.

Under the agreement, the Defendant undertook to perform the required obligations by the dates specified in Annexure A, while the Plaintiff undertook to disburse the funds in accordance with the agreed schedule. The performance timeline included a defined due date by which the Defendant was to repay the principal amount and meet any related milestones or conditions set forth in Annexure A, with the objective of completing the transaction within the agreed period.

On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff remitted Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant by bank transfer as part of the funded transaction, and payment proof is contained in Annexure B. The transfer was intended to satisfy the initial fund disbursement under the agreement, which the parties intended would commence the performance and enable the Defendant to fulfill the repayment obligation by the due date.

By the date expected under the agreement, namely {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, the Defendant failed to repay the principal amount or to comply with the performance obligations detailed in Annexure A. No payment was made on or after the due date, and the Defendant did not provide a satisfactory justification for the non-performance. The Plaintiff subsequently sought to obtain compliance by issuing repeated payment reminders and by pursuing the matter in accordance with the terms of Annexure A, with the breach arising from the Defendant’s failure to perform as agreed.

Following the breach, the Plaintiff issued a formal legal notice to the Defendant demanding immediate repayment of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} and full compliance with the agreement, as evidenced by Annexure C dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}. The Defendant replied to the notice on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, and the response is included at Annexure D. The dispute then culminated in the initiation of this suit on {{FILING_DATE}}, and the Plaintiff relies on the documents in Annexures E and F as further supporting records in connection with the claim and the asserted breach. The cause of action arose on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} in the manner described, and the Plaintiff continues to seek recovery of the claimed amount through the relief sought in this pleading.

filled_sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
filled_sections.text:
Section 65 Indian Contract Act, 1872 creates a legal obligation to restore to the person from whom a money or other advantage was received any money or property that has been obtained under a contract which has become void or which is void ab initio, recognizing that the retention of such benefit is unjust where the contract has failed in its essential terms. The triggering facts in this case are that the parties formed a business arrangement evidenced by Annexure A, under which Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} on {{DATE}} transferred Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} as consideration for the goods or services promised; Annexure B provides payment proof dated {{DATE}} confirming that the amount was paid to the Defendant; the Defendant accepted the payment and undertook obligations under the agreement, but the Defendant did not deliver the promised goods or services or confer any benefit comparable to the consideration, resulting in total failure of consideration. The contract thus either became void or is alleged to be void ab initio in view of the non-performance, such that the Defendant holds the Plaintiff’s money without lawful justification. Accordingly, the Plaintiff seeks restitution of the entire amount ₹ {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} on the basis that the Defendant obtained the money under an arrangement that has failed its essential purpose; this relief aligns with the remedial objective of restoring the status quo ante and is supported by Annexure A (the agreement) and Annexure B (payment proof). The Plaintiff also pre-empts potential defenses: there is no forfeiture clause in Annexure A justifying retention of the payment; no part performance by the Defendant can be claimed to validate keeping the money; and there is no counter-claim or set-off pleaded in Defendant’s response to the legal notice, as reflected in Annexure C (legal notice) and Annexure D (reply, if any). In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, the Plaintiff pleads restitution under the same legal framework to the extent the Court finds the contract void or rescinded in whole or in part, and prays that the Court grant such restitutionary relief as is deemed appropriate to restore the Plaintiff’s funds.

Defendant may contend that some portion of performance by the Defendant exists or that there is a lawful basis to withhold restitution; the Plaintiff denies such assertions and maintains that the record shows a total failure of consideration, with Annexure A detailing the promised performance, Annexure B demonstrating the payment by the Plaintiff, and Annexure C together with Annexure D reflecting that no adequate performance was rendered and that the defense to set-off or counter-claim has not been raised. The Plaintiff preserves the right to adduce further evidence as may be required, and submits that the protective remedy under Section 65 is appropriate to compel restoration of the money received by the Defendant under a contract that failed its essential purpose.

filled_sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
filled_sections.text: The present suit for money recovery arises from the written agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} entered into at {{PLACE_NAME}} between the Plaintiff and the Defendant, whereby the Plaintiff advanced Rs. {{AMOUNTS}} to the Defendant in reliance on the Defendant’s obligation under the contract to repay the amount. The Defendant was bound to perform and repay by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}, and on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} the Defendant failed to repay or otherwise perform the obligation despite such binding duty, thereby giving rise to the Plaintiff’s right to seek refund of Rs. {{AMOUNTS}}. The Plaintiff thereafter issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} demanding repayment, and the Defendant acknowledged receipt but, on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, refused to refund and continued to withhold the amount. The origin of the cause of action is thus anchored to the default date {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, and the cause of action further arose on {{NOTICE_DATE}} due to the Defendant’s continued failure to comply with the demand for refund after service of the notice. The Defendant continues to retain the Plaintiff’s money without any lawful authority or justification, rendering the cause of action a continuing one that subsists as of the filing date and shall endure until actual realization of the amount on the Plaintiff’s account, with the suit filed on {{FILING_DATE}} seeking recovery of the sum {{AMOUNTS}}.

filled_sections.heading: LIMITATION
filled_sections.text: The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The accrual of the cause of action occurred on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} in relation to the contract dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} between Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} and Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, whereby the Defendant failed to perform or repay as required, constituting the breach that gives rise to the Plaintiff’s claim. The default date {{DEFAULT_DATE}} marks the event that precipitated the action, and the Plaintiff filed the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} in the appropriate court, which is within the period allowed by the aforesaid Article. Accordingly, the suit is within time under the Limitation Act, 1963.

filled_sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
filled_sections.text: The suit is valued at the claim amount for the purposes of court fee, and accordingly the suit valuation equals the claim amount of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. The court fee payable on filing the suit is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}, calculated in accordance with the applicable state court fees act based on the suit valuation of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}.

filled_sections.heading: INTEREST
filled_sections.text:
Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, seeks pre-suit interest on the principal amount due under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} from the date of default by the Defendant on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} until the filing of the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} at a rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}} per annum. The principal amount due is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis is the contractual understanding between the parties and the commercial character of the transaction, whereby overdue sums attract interest as compensation for the use of money that the Defendant wrongfully retained from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} onward.

Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest on the decretable amount under Section 34 CPC from the date of filing the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} to the date of decree, at {{PENDENTE_RATE}} per annum. The amount claimed for this period is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis is Section 34 CPC; the Defendant has retained funds during the pendency of the proceedings and deprived the Plaintiff of use of its funds, and the rate is justified by the transaction being commercial and by referencing the prevailing bank lending rates for the period or the contractual understanding.

Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest on the decretal amount from the date of decree until realization at {{FUTURE_RATE}} per annum. The principal amount subjected to future interest is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis reflects the commercial nature of the transaction and seeks to compensate for the continued loss of use of funds post-decree, aligned with the contractual understanding.

validation_issues.description: Claim type 'payment' requires evidence but has no evidence_refs

filled_sections.heading: PRAYER
filled_sections.text:
(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) being the principal amount advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}.
(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} to {{FILING_DATE}}.
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDETE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} till the date of decree.
(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree until realization.
(e) Award costs of the suit including the pre-litigation costs incurred for sending the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and postal charges, total Rs. {{COSTS_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{COSTS_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only).
(f) Grant such other relief as the Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

filled_sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
filled_sections.text:
1. Serial No.: 1; Description: The Plaintiff relies on the Agreement/Contract document between the Plaintiff and Defendant, evidenced by Annexure A, executed on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}} and governing the underlying transaction; Annexure Label: Annexure A.
2. Serial No.: 2; Description: The Plaintiff relies on payment proof in the form of bank statement/receipt/UTR, as per Annexure B, showing payments of {{AMOUNT}} made by Plaintiff to Defendant on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}} via {{MODE}}; Annexure Label: Annexure B.
3. Serial No.: 3; Description: The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to Defendant dated {{DATE}} at {{ADDRESS}} through {{MODE}}, with copy annexed as Annexure C; Annexure Label: Annexure C.
4. Serial No.: 4; Description: The Defendant's reply to the legal notice, if any, dated {{DATE}} and served at {{LOCATION}} through {{MODE}}, is included as Annexure D; Annexure Label: Annexure D.
5. Serial No.: 5; Description: The Plaintiff relies on the Acknowledgment/Admission document, if any, by Defendant, dated {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}}, annexed as Annexure E; Annexure Label: Annexure E.
6. Serial No.: 6; Description: Additional supporting documents referenced by the Plaintiff in connection with this claim, including relevant communications and records, are attached as Annexure F; Annexure Label: Annexure F.

filled_sections.heading: VERIFICATION
filled_sections.text:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

draft_artifacts.title: SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE {{COURT_NAME}}
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / {{SUIT_YEAR}}

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)


BETWEEN

Between Plaintiff and Defendant, the Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}, is a sui juris person competent to sue and be sued. The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, is a sui juris person competent to be sued.


JURISDICTION

The territorial jurisdiction of this Court is founded on direct, concrete connections between the parties and the events giving rise to the dispute. The Defendant resides and carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which lies within the territorial jurisdiction of this Court. The advance amount was transferred by the Plaintiff from its account at {{BANK_NAME}} {{BANK_BRANCH}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, within the local limits of this Court's jurisdiction. The agreement was executed at {{AGREEMENT_PLACE}} on {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, and the contract is performed within the same territorial footprint. The cause of action arose at {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_PLACE}} on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} when the Defendant allegedly defaulted on repayment and performance. Place: {{PLACE}}.

Pecuniary jurisdiction is invoked for recovery of the money claim. The Plaintiff claims Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}, which falls within this Court's monetary limits.


FACTS OF THE CASE

Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, and Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, first became acquainted in the course of their regular commercial dealings, and subsequently entered into a written contract dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} (Annexure A) under which the Plaintiff agreed to furnish funds to the Defendant for a stated business purpose and the Defendant agreed to accept the funds and perform the obligations set out in Annexure A. The relationship between the parties thus was that of a financier and borrower arising from a formalized financing arrangement documented in Annexure A.

The terms of the agreement, as reflected in Annexure A, contemplated that the Plaintiff would advance funds to the Defendant for the specified purpose and that the Defendant would repay the principal amount on the predetermined due date and adhere to the other conditions described in Annexure A, including any remedies available on breach. The contract also contained the condition that all terms would be binding and enforceable against the parties in the event of non-performance.

Under the agreement, the Defendant undertook to perform the required obligations by the dates specified in Annexure A, while the Plaintiff undertook to disburse the funds in accordance with the agreed schedule. The performance timeline included a defined due date by which the Defendant was to repay the principal amount and meet any related milestones or conditions set forth in Annexure A, with the objective of completing the transaction within the agreed period.

On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff remitted Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant by bank transfer as part of the funded transaction, and payment proof is contained in Annexure B. The transfer was intended to satisfy the initial fund disbursement under the agreement, which the parties intended would commence the performance and enable the Defendant to fulfill the repayment obligation by the due date.

By the date expected under the agreement, namely {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, the Defendant failed to repay the principal amount or to comply with the performance obligations detailed in Annexure A. No payment was made on or after the due date, and the Defendant did not provide a satisfactory justification for the non-performance. The Plaintiff subsequently sought to obtain compliance by issuing repeated payment reminders and by pursuing the matter in accordance with the terms of Annexure A, with the breach arising from the Defendant’s failure to perform as agreed.

Following the breach, the Plaintiff issued a formal legal notice to the Defendant demanding immediate repayment of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} and full compliance with the agreement, as evidenced by Annexure C dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}. The Defendant replied to the notice on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, and the response is included at Annexure D. The dispute then culminated in the initiation of this suit on {{FILING_DATE}}, and the Plaintiff relies on the documents in Annexures E and F as further supporting records in connection with the claim and the asserted breach. The cause of action arose on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} in the manner described, and the Plaintiff continues to seek recovery of the claimed amount through the relief sought in this pleading.


LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

Section 65 Indian Contract Act, 1872 creates a legal obligation to restore to the person from whom a money or other advantage was received any money or property that has been obtained under a contract which has become void or which is void ab initio, recognizing that the retention of such benefit is unjust where the contract has failed in its essential terms. The triggering facts in this case are that the parties formed a business arrangement evidenced by Annexure A, under which Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} on {{DATE}} transferred Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} as consideration for the goods or services promised; Annexure B provides payment proof dated {{DATE}} confirming that the amount was paid to the Defendant; the Defendant accepted the payment and undertook obligations under the agreement, but the Defendant did not deliver the promised goods or services or confer any benefit comparable to the consideration, resulting in total failure of consideration. The contract thus either became void or is alleged to be void ab initio in view of the non-performance, such that the Defendant holds the Plaintiff’s money without lawful justification. Accordingly, the Plaintiff seeks restitution of the entire amount ₹ {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} on the basis that the Defendant obtained the money under an arrangement that has failed its essential purpose; this relief aligns with the remedial objective of restoring the status quo ante and is supported by Annexure A (the agreement) and Annexure B (payment proof). The Plaintiff also pre-empts potential defenses: there is no forfeiture clause in Annexure A justifying retention of the payment; no part performance by the Defendant can be claimed to validate keeping the money; and there is no counter-claim or set-off pleaded in Defendant’s response to the legal notice, as reflected in Annexure C (legal notice) and Annexure D (reply, if any). In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, the Plaintiff pleads restitution under the same legal framework to the extent the Court finds the contract void or rescinded in whole or in part, and prays that the Court grant such restitutionary relief as is deemed appropriate to restore the Plaintiff’s funds.

Defendant may contend that some portion of performance by the Defendant exists or that there is a lawful basis to withhold restitution; the Plaintiff denies such assertions and maintains that the record shows a total failure of consideration, with Annexure A detailing the promised performance, Annexure B demonstrating the payment by the Plaintiff, and Annexure C together with Annexure D reflecting that no adequate performance was rendered and that the defense to set-off or counter-claim has not been raised. The Plaintiff preserves the right to adduce further evidence as may be required, and submits that the protective remedy under Section 65 is appropriate to compel restoration of the money received by the Defendant under a contract that failed its essential purpose.


CAUSE OF ACTION

The present suit for money recovery arises from the written agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} entered into at {{PLACE_NAME}} between the Plaintiff and the Defendant, whereby the Plaintiff advanced Rs. {{AMOUNTS}} to the Defendant in reliance on the Defendant’s obligation under the contract to repay the amount. The Defendant was bound to perform and repay by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}, and on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} the Defendant failed to repay or otherwise perform the obligation despite such binding duty, thereby giving rise to the Plaintiff’s right to seek refund of Rs. {{AMOUNTS}}. The Plaintiff thereafter issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} demanding repayment, and the Defendant acknowledged receipt but, on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, refused to refund and continued to withhold the amount. The origin of the cause of action is thus anchored to the default date {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, and the cause of action further arose on {{NOTICE_DATE}} due to the Defendant’s continued failure to comply with the demand for refund after service of the notice. The Defendant continues to retain the Plaintiff’s money without any lawful authority or justification, rendering the cause of action a continuing one that subsists as of the filing date and shall endure until actual realization of the amount on the Plaintiff’s account, with the suit filed on {{FILING_DATE}} seeking recovery of the sum {{AMOUNTS}}.


LIMITATION

The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The accrual of the cause of action occurred on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} in relation to the contract dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} between Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} and Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, whereby the Defendant failed to perform or repay as required, constituting the breach that gives rise to the Plaintiff’s claim. The default date {{DEFAULT_DATE}} marks the event that precipitated the action, and the Plaintiff filed the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} in the appropriate court, which is within the period allowed by the aforesaid Article. Accordingly, the suit is within time under the Limitation Act, 1963.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The suit is valued at the claim amount for the purposes of court fee, and accordingly the suit valuation equals the claim amount of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. The court fee payable on filing the suit is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}, calculated in accordance with the applicable state court fees act based on the suit valuation of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}.


INTEREST

Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, seeks pre-suit interest on the principal amount due under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} from the date of default by the Defendant on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} until the filing of the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} at a rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}} per annum. The principal amount due is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis is the contractual understanding between the parties and the commercial character of the transaction, whereby overdue sums attract interest as compensation for the use of money that the Defendant wrongfully retained from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} onward.

Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest on the decretable amount under Section 34 CPC from the date of filing the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} to the date of decree, at {{PENDENTE_RATE}} per annum. The amount claimed for this period is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis is Section 34 CPC; the Defendant has retained funds during the pendency of the proceedings and deprived the Plaintiff of use of its funds, and the rate is justified by the transaction being commercial and by referencing the prevailing bank lending rates for the period or the contractual understanding.

Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest on the decretal amount from the date of decree until realization at {{FUTURE_RATE}} per annum. The principal amount subjected to future interest is {{AMOUNT_DUE}}. The legal basis reflects the commercial nature of the transaction and seeks to compensate for the continued loss of use of funds post-decree, aligned with the contractual understanding.


PRAYER

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) being the principal amount advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}.
(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} to {{FILING_DATE}}.
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDETE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} till the date of decree.
(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree until realization.
(e) Award costs of the suit including the pre-litigation costs incurred for sending the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and postal charges, total Rs. {{COSTS_AMOUNT}} (Rupees {{COSTS_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only).
(f) Grant such other relief as the Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

1. Serial No.: 1; Description: The Plaintiff relies on the Agreement/Contract document between the Plaintiff and Defendant, evidenced by Annexure A, executed on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}} and governing the underlying transaction; Annexure Label: Annexure A.
2. Serial No.: 2; Description: The Plaintiff relies on payment proof in the form of bank statement/receipt/UTR, as per Annexure B, showing payments of {{AMOUNT}} made by Plaintiff to Defendant on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}} via {{MODE}}; Annexure Label: Annexure B.
3. Serial No.: 3; Description: The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to Defendant dated {{DATE}} at {{ADDRESS}} through {{MODE}}, with copy annexed as Annexure C; Annexure Label: Annexure C.
4. Serial No.: 4; Description: The Defendant's reply to the legal notice, if any, dated {{DATE}} and served at {{LOCATION}} through {{MODE}}, is included as Annexure D; Annexure Label: Annexure D.
5. Serial No.: 5; Description: The Plaintiff relies on the Acknowledgment/Admission document, if any, by Defendant, dated {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}}, annexed as Annexure E; Annexure Label: Annexure E.
6. Serial No.: 6; Description: Additional supporting documents referenced by the Plaintiff in connection with this claim, including relevant communications and records, are attached as Annexure F; Annexure Label: Annexure F.


VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No.: {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
Place: {{COURT_PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}
